authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for cGMP, preventive controls, monitoring, corrective actions, verification, supply-chain programs, and recordkeeping in human food manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's rule page is the official workflow overview for covered food facilities and clarifies applicability, written food safety plan posture, and related implementation references used by plant-compliance teams.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for traceability lot codes, critical tracking event records, and FDA-requested retrieval timing for covered foods.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official FAQ set is authoritative workflow guidance for current implementation timing, applicability questions, and record-sharing expectations, including the extended compliance date of July 20, 2028.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance is authoritative public workflow guidance for supplier approval, verification activity selection, receiving procedures, and co-manufacturer handling under the preventive-controls rules.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when unresolved hold, release, or nonconformance questions must escalate into recall-ready or regulator-facing handling.

authoritative_sources.title: General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Codex is the globally recognized public baseline for food hygiene and HACCP vocabulary, including operator responsibilities, verification, documentation, training, and corrective action expectations used across food manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code that is directly relevant to documented procedures, nonconforming product handling, verification records, and plant-level audit readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's public traceability standard is a recognized vendor-neutral workflow reference for linking lot, receipt, shipment, and transformation data across supply-chain systems.

workflow_stages.name: request intake and plant-scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify plant, line, product family, jurisdiction, lot scope, supplier context, and requested decision before any compliance recommendation is prepared.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map cGMP, preventive-controls, supplier-verification, traceability, sanitation, hold, release, and change-control requirements to the exact records and owners needed for the work item.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and review readiness
workflow_stages.description: Collect and compare batch, lot, inventory, supplier, sanitation, verification, nonconformance, and release artifacts needed to support a bounded compliance conclusion.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability, supplier, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible genealogy, inventory, supplier verification, hold status, and release prerequisites support traceable handling or require blocked escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, remediation, and inspection preparation
workflow_stages.description: Translate missing evidence, policy-versus-practice drift, supplier issues, hold conflicts, and audit questions into remediation and inspection-ready packets without claiming final signoff authority.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, batch records, batch reconciliation records, and lot genealogy logs
inventory states, warehouse movements, quarantine or hold records, and release records
food safety plans, preventive-control monitoring and verification records, sanitation SOPs, pre-op checks, and internal audit findings
nonconformance reports, CAPA records, deviation investigations, and change-control packages
supplier approval records, receiving logs, COAs, supplier corrective-action responses, and specification updates
mock recall packets, shipment records, consignee lists, and traceability retrieval exports
inspection preparation notes, evidence checklists, and owner-routed remediation summaries

allowed: classify plant-compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, evidence-gap summaries, inspection-ready packets, and remediation escalations tied to named artifacts and owners
determine whether visible control obligations and evidence are satisfied, missing, stale, or in conflict without improvising final release or legal authority
escalate: required tenant facts, jurisdiction context, release ownership, or source-of-truth mappings are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, customer, or tenant-policy conflicts materially change traceability, hold, release, supplier, or recall posture
traceability, hold, supplier-verification, or sanitation evidence gaps increase release, safety, or regulator-facing risk
requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal signoff, final release authority, or supplier approval
refuse: requests for final legal signoff, guaranteed regulator acceptance, or final release approval
requests to proceed with release, substitution, rework, or shipment when required batch, lot, supplier, hold, or sanitation evidence is missing or contradictory
requests that collapse the lane into generic compliance administration and ignore food-manufacturing traceability, supplier, or release realities

domain_failure_modes: Allowing release or shipment to proceed even though traceability, hold, or supplier-verification evidence is missing or contradictory.
Treating batch-record corrections or temporary hold removal as clerical cleanup instead of a controlled compliance event.
Applying a tenant SOP or customer expectation that conflicts with the public regulatory baseline without escalating the conflict.
Treating supplier substitutions or COA mismatches as routine procurement issues even though supplier approval and release boundaries are unresolved.
Producing a confident compliance conclusion without named systems of record, accountable evidence owners, or current release authority context.
Collapsing plant compliance into legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or final quality disposition that belongs to adjacent owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff

notes: The public baseline is anchored on FDA human-food manufacturing sources. Product categories that fall under USDA-FSIS or other jurisdiction-specific regimes must be supplied through tenant retrieval and routed to the correct owner when they materially change the answer.
The specialist is intentionally structured as plant-compliance support labor and must not imply final legal, quality-release, supplier-approval, or regulator-facing authority.
